title: Eastern European Summer Time

infobox.value: [Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00"))
[Western European Time](/wiki/Western_European_Time "Western European Time") / [Greenwich Mean Time](/wiki/Greenwich_Mean_Time "Greenwich Mean Time") ([UTC](/wiki/UTC%2B00:00 "UTC+00:00"))

infobox.key: [Western European Summer Time](/wiki/Western_European_Summer_Time "Western European Summer Time") / [British Summer Time](/wiki/British_Summer_Time "British Summer Time") / [Irish Standard Time](/wiki/Time_in_the_Republic_of_Ireland "Time in the Republic of Ireland") ([UTC+1](/wiki/UTC%2B01:00 "UTC+01:00"))
[Central European Summer Time](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time") ([UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"))
infobox.value: | Yellow | [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time") / [Kaliningrad Time](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Time "Kaliningrad Time") ([UTC+2](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00"))
| Green | [Moscow Time](/wiki/Moscow_Time "Moscow Time") / [Turkey Time](/wiki/Time_in_Turkey "Time in Turkey") ([UTC+3](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00"))

[Armenia Time](/wiki/Armenia_Time "Armenia Time") / [Azerbaijan Time](/wiki/Azerbaijan_Time "Azerbaijan Time") / [Georgia Time](/wiki/Georgia_Time "Georgia Time") / [Samara Time](/wiki/Samara_Time "Samara Time") ([UTC+4](/wiki/UTC%2B04:00 "UTC+04:00"))
Legal time vs. local mean time

(**EEST**) is one of the names of the [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone"), which is 3 hours ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time"). It is used as a summer [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") in some [European](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and [Middle Eastern](/wiki/Middle_East "Middle East") countries, which makes it the same as [Arabia Standard Time](/wiki/Arabia_Standard_Time "Arabia Standard Time"), [East Africa Time](/wiki/East_Africa_Time "East Africa Time"), and [Moscow Time](/wiki/Moscow_Time "Moscow Time"). During the winter periods, [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time") ([UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) is used.
Joseph Myers (2009-07-17). ["History of legal time in Britain"](http://www.srcf.ucam.org/~jsm28/british-time/). Retrieved 2009-10-11.
[Ukraine to return to standard time on Oct. 30 (updated)](http://www.kyivpost.com/news/nation/detail/115146/), [Kyiv Post](/wiki/Kyiv_Post "Kyiv Post") (October 18, 2011)

["Про порядок денний одинадцятої сесії Верховної Ради України дев'ятого скликання"](https://zakon.rada.gov.ua/go/3562-20). *Офіційний вебпортал парламенту України* (in Ukrainian). Retrieved 2024-07-17.

| Turquoise | [Armenia Time](/wiki/Armenia_Time "Armenia Time") / [Azerbaijan Time](/wiki/Azerbaijan_Time "Azerbaijan Time") / [Georgia Time](/wiki/Georgia_Time "Georgia Time") / [Samara Time](/wiki/Samara_Time "Samara Time") ([UTC+4](/wiki/UTC%2B04:00 "UTC+04:00")) |

▉▉▉▉ Pale hues: Standard time observed all year   
   ▉▉▉   Dark hues: [Daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time")

**Eastern European Summer Time** (**EEST**) is one of the names of the [UTC+03:00](/wiki/UTC%2B03:00 "UTC+03:00") [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone"), which is 3 hours ahead of [Coordinated Universal Time](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time"). It is used as a summer [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") in some [European](/wiki/Europe "Europe") and [Middle Eastern](/wiki/Middle_East "Middle East") countries, which makes it the same as [Arabia Standard Time](/wiki/Arabia_Standard_Time "Arabia Standard Time"), [East Africa Time](/wiki/East_Africa_Time "East Africa Time"), and [Moscow Time](/wiki/Moscow_Time "Moscow Time"). During the winter periods, [Eastern European Time](/wiki/Eastern_European_Time "Eastern European Time") ([UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00")) is used.

Since 1996, [European Summer Time](/wiki/European_Summer_Time "European Summer Time") has been applied from the last Sunday in March to the last Sunday in October. Previously, the rules were not uniform across the European Union.[[1]](#cite_note-Myers-1)

## Usage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eastern_European_Summer_Time&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Usage")]

The following countries and territories use Eastern European Summer Time during the summer:

* [Belarus](/wiki/Belarus "Belarus"), [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in years 1981–89, regular EEST from 1991-2011
* [Bulgaria](/wiki/Bulgaria "Bulgaria"), regular EEST since 1979
* [Cyprus](/wiki/Cyprus "Cyprus"), regular EEST since 1979 ([Northern Cyprus](/wiki/Northern_Cyprus "Northern Cyprus") stopped using EEST in September 2016, but returned to EEST in March 2018[[2]](#cite_note-2))
* [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt"), in the years 1988–2010, 2014–2015 and since 2023 (see also [Egypt Standard Time](/wiki/Egypt_Standard_Time "Egypt Standard Time"))
* [Estonia](/wiki/Estonia "Estonia"), [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in years 1981–88, regular EEST since 1989
* [Finland](/wiki/Finland "Finland"), regular EEST since 1981
* [Greece](/wiki/Greece "Greece"), regular EEST since 1975
* [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel"), [Israel Daylight Time](/wiki/Israel_Daylight_Time "Israel Daylight Time") since 1948 (which tracks EEST when the two overlap)
* [Jordan](/wiki/Jordan "Jordan"), since 1985 (permanent DST since 2022)
* [Latvia](/wiki/Latvia "Latvia"), [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in years 1981–88, regular EEST since 1989
* [Lebanon](/wiki/Lebanon "Lebanon"), since 1984
* [Lithuania](/wiki/Lithuania "Lithuania"), [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in years 1981–88, regular EEST since 1989, apart from in years 1998-2003 when it was [Central European Summer Time](/wiki/Central_European_Summer_Time "Central European Summer Time")
* [Moldova](/wiki/Moldova "Moldova"), [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in years 1932–40 and 1981–89, regular EEST since 1991
* [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"), unofficial EEST in years 1932–40, regular EEST since 1979
* [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") ([Kaliningrad](/wiki/Kaliningrad_Oblast "Kaliningrad Oblast")), [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in years 1981–90, regular EEST since 1991, as standard time in 2011-2014.
* [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria"), since 1983 (permanent DST since 2022)
* [Ukraine](/wiki/Ukraine "Ukraine"), [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in years 1981–89, regular EEST from 1992[[3]](#cite_note-Kyivwintertimeisback-3) to 2024.[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4)

In 1991, EEST was used also in [Moscow](/wiki/Moscow "Moscow") and [Samara](/wiki/Samara_Oblast "Samara Oblast") time zones of [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"). [Egypt](/wiki/Egypt "Egypt") has previously used EEST in 1957–2010 and 2014–2015. [Turkey](/wiki/Turkey "Turkey"), has previously used EEST in 1970–1978, EEST and [Moscow Summer Time](/wiki/Moscow_Summer_Time "Moscow Summer Time") in 1979–1983, and EEST in 1985–2016.

| Colour | Legal time vs. local mean time |
| --- | --- |
|  | 1 h ± 30 m behind |
|  | 0 h ± 30 m |
|  | 1 h ± 30 m ahead |
|  | 2 h ± 30 m ahead |
|  | 3 h ± 30 m ahead |
